# Pionex.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the Pionex REST and WebSocket APIs. Pionex.Net currently exposes Spot trading and is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem.

Pionex.Net provides typed REST clients, WebSocket subscriptions with automatic connection management, HMAC credentials through `PionexCredentials`, dependency injection helpers, local order books, user client management, Native AOT support, and exchange-agnostic CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis. It targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, and net10.0.

Direct and SharedApis REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` or `HttpResult`. Direct and SharedApis WebSocket subscriptions return `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`. Always check `.Success` before accessing `.Data`. Pionex symbols use an underscore, for example `BTC_USDT`.

## Critical Usage Rules

- Use `PionexRestClient` for REST and `PionexSocketClient` for subscriptions.
- Configure private clients with `new PionexCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET")`.
- Route REST calls through `SpotApi.ExchangeData`, `SpotApi.Account`, or `SpotApi.Trading`.
- Route all direct WebSocket subscriptions through `socketClient.SpotApi`.
- Pionex.Net currently has no futures, margin, options, or derivatives API client.
- `GetTickersAsync` and `GetBookTickersAsync` return arrays even when filtering one symbol.
- For market buys, pass `quoteQuantity`; for market sells, pass `quantity`.
- Private socket subscriptions use credentials on `PionexSocketClient`; there is no listen-key workflow.
- Store successful `UpdateSubscription` values and unsubscribe with `UnsubscribeAsync`.
- Use `.SpotApi.SharedClient` for CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis code and `Discover()` for runtime capability metadata.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/README.md): Overview, installation, features, and basic usage
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/llms-full.txt): Detailed endpoint routing, patterns, pitfalls, and anti-hallucination rules
- [Assistant Guide](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/AGENTS.md): Compact code-generation guidance
- [CryptoExchange.Net Documentation](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/client-libs/getting-started): Shared client concepts, result processing, dependency injection, and WebSocket behavior

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/tree/main/Examples/ai-friendly): Self-contained examples optimized for coding assistants
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Public ticker/order book and authenticated balances
- [Spot Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/02-spot-trading.cs): Limit order placement, lookup, and cancellation
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Public trades/order book, private balances, and teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): `HttpResult<T>`, error metadata, and transient retry
- [Full examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/tree/main/Examples): Console, API, order placement, order book, and tracker projects

## Reference

- [NuGet package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Pionex.Net)
- [Pionex API documentation](https://www.pionex.com/docs/api-docs)
- [CryptoExchange.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net)
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net)
- [GitHub issues](https://github.com/JKorf/Pionex.Net/issues)
